title: Acetosella bifolia
intro: Acetosella bifolia, also known as the two-leafed sorrel, is a perennial plant belonging to the family Oxalidaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and indigestion, due to its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The bioactive constituents of Acetosella bifolia include flavonoids, tannins, and oxalic acid, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Acetosella bifolia are teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to alleviate gastrointestinal discomfort and promote healthy digestion.
